reasoning_log.message: Demo baseline loaded from comprehensive enterprise fixture (30 techniques).
Coverage posture reconstructed from simulated Splunk MITRE compliance lookup and ARIA memory state.
Attack profiles pre-seeded for prioritized GAP techniques based on ATT&CK tactic context.

T1566.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages with malicious links or attachments to gain initial access.
T1566.detection: Monitor inbound email metadata, suspicious sender domains, and attachment execution follow-on events.

attack_profile.description: Campaigns commonly use lure themes (invoice, payroll, MFA reset) followed by credential harvesting or malware delivery.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Correlate suspicious sender traits with malicious URL click-through and process creation on recipient endpoints.

T1566.generated_rule: search index=email sourcetype=o365:message traceDirection=Inbound (subject="*invoice*" OR subject="*payment*" OR subject="*secure document*") | eval sender_domain=lower(replace(sender,".*@","")) | lookup known_sender_domains domain as sender_domain OUTPUTNEW reputation as sender_reputation | where isnull(sender_reputation) OR sender_reputation="new" OR sender_reputation="suspicious" | stats count values(subject) as subjects values(urls) as urls dc(recipient) as recipients by sender sender_domain reply_to | where count>=5 OR recipients>=3
T1566.rule_explanation: Detects likely phishing bursts by combining lure subjects with sender-domain novelty and recipient spread.

T1190.description: Adversaries may exploit internet-facing applications to gain a foothold.
T1190.detection: Monitor WAF and web server logs for exploit signatures, path traversal, and anomalous response codes.

attack_profile.description: Exploit attempts target externally exposed apps and often precede webshell placement and lateral movement.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Flag concentrated exploit-like URI patterns with high 4xx/5xx ratios per source IP.

T1190.generated_rule: search index=web (sourcetype=nginx OR sourcetype=apache OR sourcetype=waf) | eval uri_l=lower(uri) | eval exploit_hit=if(match(uri_l,"(\.\./|union\s+select|cmd=|/wp-admin|/cgi-bin/|jndi:|\${jndi|/etc/passwd)"),1,0) | where exploit_hit=1 OR status>=500 | stats count values(uri) as uris values(user_agent) as user_agents dc(uri) as unique_uris by src_ip dest status | where count>=8 OR unique_uris>=6
T1190.rule_explanation: Identifies concentrated exploit behavior against public apps by combining exploit-like request artifacts and error responses.

T1055.description: Adversaries may inject code into processes to evade defenses and execute in trusted contexts.
T1055.detection: Monitor suspicious API call patterns and parent-child process anomalies from EDR/Sysmon telemetry.

attack_profile.description: Threat actors inject shellcode or DLL payloads into benign processes to blend execution.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Track process access rights, remote thread creation, and suspicious process ancestry.

T1055.generated_rule: search index=endpoint (sourcetype=sysmon:process OR sourcetype=sysmon:access) | eval image_l=lower(coalesce(Image, process_name)) | eval target_l=lower(coalesce(TargetImage, target_process_name)) | eval inject_indicator=if(match(lower(coalesce(CallTrace,GrantedAccess,EventDescription)),"(writeprocessmemory|createremotethread|virtualallocex|process injection)"),1,0) | where inject_indicator=1 OR (target_l="*lsass.exe*" AND match(GrantedAccess,"0x1f0fff|0x1fffff")) | stats count values(image_l) as src_process values(target_l) as target_process values(CommandLine) as cmdlines by host user
T1055.rule_explanation: Detects likely process injection behavior via high-risk memory/thread primitives and privileged LSASS access rights.

T1003.description: Adversaries may dump credentials from operating systems and memory stores.
T1003.detection: Alert on LSASS memory access, suspicious credential tooling, and SAM/SECURITY hive access.

attack_profile.description: Credential dumping is frequently observed before privilege escalation and lateral movement.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Focus on process command-line patterns and sensitive-process access telemetry.

T1003.generated_rule: search index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon:process | eval cmd_l=lower(coalesce(CommandLine, process)) | where match(cmd_l,"(mimikatz|sekurlsa|lsadump|procdump\s+.*lsass|comsvcs\.dll,\s*minidump|rundll32\s+.*comsvcs)") OR (match(cmd_l,"reg\s+save") AND (match(cmd_l,"\bhklm\\sam\b") OR match(cmd_l,"\bhklm\\security\b"))) | stats count values(Image) as image values(CommandLine) as command by host user parent_process
T1003.rule_explanation: Finds common credential-dumping tradecraft using process command lines tied to LSASS and registry hive theft.

T1048.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data using protocols not typically used for enterprise data transfer.
T1048.detection: Detect anomalous high-volume outbound transfers over uncommon ports/protocols per host role.

attack_profile.description: Data theft may use FTP, WebDAV, cloud sync abuse, or custom protocols to bypass controls.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Baseline outbound destinations and alert on sustained transfer spikes to low-prevalence external endpoints.

T1048.generated_rule: search index=network (sourcetype=zeek:conn OR sourcetype=pan:traffic OR sourcetype=proxy) direction=outbound | eval bytes_out=coalesce(bytes_out, out_bytes, bytes_sent, 0) | eval dest_port=coalesce(dest_port, dpt, port) | where isnotnull(dest_ip) | stats sum(bytes_out) as total_out count dc(dest_port) as unique_ports values(dest_port) as ports values(app) as apps by src_ip dest_ip user | where total_out>200000000 AND (unique_ports>=3 OR NOT match(mvjoin(ports,","),"443|80|53"))
T1048.rule_explanation: Flags likely exfiltration by identifying unusually large outbound transfers to external destinations over non-standard service patterns.

T1486.description: Adversaries may encrypt data to disrupt business operations and demand ransom.
T1486.detection: Look for rapid file modification bursts, shadow copy deletion, and suspicious encryption utility execution.

attack_profile.description: Ransomware operations commonly pair data encryption with defense-evasion and recovery inhibition techniques.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Correlate high-rate file rename/write activity with backup tampering and known ransomware command lines.

T1486.generated_rule: search index=endpoint (sourcetype=sysmon:file OR sourcetype=sysmon:process) | eval cmd_l=lower(coalesce(CommandLine, process_name)) | eval ransomware_cli=if(match(cmd_l,"(vssadmin\s+delete\s+shadows|wmic\s+shadowcopy\s+delete|bcdedit\s+/set\s+\{default\}\s+recoveryenabled\s+no)"),1,0) | eval file_ext=lower(coalesce(TargetFilename, file_path)) | stats count(eval(match(file_ext,"\.(locked|crypted|enc|encrypted|ryk)$"))) as suspect_file_events max(ransomware_cli) as cli_seen values(process_name) as processes by host user | where suspect_file_events>=40 OR cli_seen=1
T1486.rule_explanation: Detects likely ransomware impact behavior using encryption-extension spikes and destructive backup/recovery commands.

T1027.description: Adversaries may obfuscate malware, scripts, or commands to evade detection.
T1027.detection: Detect encoded command execution, unusual archive usage, and script obfuscation artifacts.

attack_profile.description: Malicious payloads often rely on encoding or compression to reduce static detection efficacy.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Look for encoded PowerShell, certutil decode patterns, and suspicious archive extraction to temp paths.

T1027.generated_rule: search index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon:process | eval cmd_l=lower(coalesce(CommandLine, process)) | where match(cmd_l,"(\-enc\s|encodedcommand|frombase64string|certutil\s+\-decode|powershell\s+\-nop\s+\-w\s+hidden|7z\s+x\s+.*\\temp)") | stats count values(Image) as image values(ParentImage) as parent values(CommandLine) as cmd by host user
T1027.rule_explanation: Highlights common obfuscation tradecraft in process command lines, especially encoded script execution and decode utilities.

T1105.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or payloads from external systems into compromised environments.
T1105.detection: Monitor download utilities and script engines retrieving binaries from suspicious hosts.

attack_profile.description: Attackers frequently stage second-phase tooling via LOLBins and script-based downloaders.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Track process + network telemetry for native download utilities writing executables or scripts.

T1105.generated_rule: search index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon:process | eval cmd_l=lower(coalesce(CommandLine, process)) | where match(cmd_l,"(invoke-webrequest|downloadstring\(|bitsadmin\s+/transfer|certutil\s+\-urlcache\s+\-split\s+\-f|curl\s+https?://|wget\s+https?://)") | eval writes_payload=if(match(cmd_l,"(\.exe|\.dll|\.ps1|\.vbs|\.js)"),1,0) | stats count max(writes_payload) as writes_payload values(Image) as image values(CommandLine) as cmds by host user parent_process | where writes_payload=1 OR count>=3
T1105.rule_explanation: Finds likely ingress transfer behavior using common download primitives and payload file write indicators.

T1218.description: Adversaries may abuse signed system binaries to proxy execution of malicious code.
T1218.detection: Alert on LOLBin command patterns (rundll32, regsvr32, mshta, installutil) with remote/script payloads.

attack_profile.description: Threat actors exploit trusted binaries to evade application control and signature-based detection.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Identify suspicious argument patterns and script/remote URL invocation by trusted binaries.

T1218.generated_rule: search index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon:process | eval image_l=lower(coalesce(Image, process_name)) | eval cmd_l=lower(coalesce(CommandLine, process)) | where (match(image_l,"(rundll32\.exe|regsvr32\.exe|mshta\.exe|installutil\.exe)") AND match(cmd_l,"(http://|https://|javascript:|scrobj\.dll|\.sct|\.hta|\.dll,|/u\s+/i:)")) | stats count values(Image) as image values(ParentImage) as parent values(CommandLine) as command by host user
T1218.rule_explanation: Detects probable LOLBin proxy execution by matching signed binaries with known suspicious execution arguments.

T1112.description: Adversaries may modify registry values for persistence, execution, or defense evasion.
T1112.detection: Monitor sensitive autorun, security, and policy keys for suspicious changes.

attack_profile.description: Registry tampering supports persistence and can weaken security controls before payload execution.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Focus on high-risk key paths and correlate with unusual process lineage.

T1112.generated_rule: search index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon:registry | eval target_l=lower(coalesce(TargetObject, registry_path)) | where match(target_l,"(\\software\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\run|\\software\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\runonce|\\system\\currentcontrolset\\services|\\policies\\microsoft\\windows defender)") | stats count values(Image) as image values(TargetObject) as keys values(Details) as values by host user EventType
T1112.rule_explanation: Surfaces suspicious modifications to persistence and security-relevant registry paths often abused by malware.

T1555.description: Adversaries may attempt to extract credentials from browsers and local password stores.
T1555.detection: Monitor browser credential DB access and suspicious process reads of login data files.

attack_profile.description: Stealers target browser credential stores and cookies to hijack sessions and accounts.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Alert on non-browser processes reading browser credential store files or vault APIs.

T1555.generated_rule: search index=endpoint (sourcetype=sysmon:file OR sourcetype=sysmon:process) | eval target_l=lower(coalesce(TargetFilename, file_path, CommandLine)) | where match(target_l,"(\\appdata\\local\\google\\chrome\\user data\\default\\login data|\\appdata\\roaming\\mozilla\\firefox\\profiles|\\cookies\")") AND NOT match(lower(coalesce(Image, process_name)),"(chrome\.exe|msedge\.exe|firefox\.exe)") | stats count values(Image) as image values(TargetFilename) as targets values(CommandLine) as cmd by host user
T1555.rule_explanation: Detects likely credential theft by identifying non-browser processes touching browser password/cookie stores.

T1552.description: Adversaries may search for credentials in files, scripts, or configuration artifacts.
T1552.detection: Watch for command patterns searching for password/token strings in sensitive directories.

attack_profile.description: Operators often discover plaintext credentials in scripts, config files, and deployment artifacts.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Detect broad credential keyword hunting over user profile and config directories.

T1552.generated_rule: search index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon:process | eval cmd_l=lower(coalesce(CommandLine, process)) | where match(cmd_l,"(findstr|select-string|grep|ripgrep)") AND match(cmd_l,"(password|passwd|token|apikey|secret|connectionstring)") AND match(cmd_l,"(users|appdata|programdata|\.config|\.env|backup|scripts)") | stats count values(Image) as image values(CommandLine) as cmds by host user parent_process
T1552.rule_explanation: Highlights credential-hunting behavior using string-search utilities over likely secret-bearing locations.

T1053.description: Adversaries may abuse scheduled tasks or cron to execute payloads persistently.
T1053.detection: Audit new task creation and suspicious task actions referencing script interpreters.

attack_profile.description: Task scheduling provides durable execution with elevated context in many intrusions.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Capture both task creation and suspicious executable/script targets.

T1547.description: Adversaries may configure autostart extensibility points to persist execution.
T1547.detection: Track registry and startup folder modifications tied to unusual binaries.

attack_profile.description: Autostart abuse is common post-compromise persistence behavior.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Baseline startup entries and detect low-prevalence additions.

T1110.description: Adversaries may attempt repeated authentication guesses to gain valid credentials.
T1110.detection: Detect repeated failed logons and distributed password spray patterns.

attack_profile.description: Brute force can be noisy or distributed, often preceding privileged access abuse.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Correlate user and source-IP failure velocity with lockout outcomes.

T1570.description: Adversaries may transfer tools between internal systems to continue operations.
T1570.detection: Monitor SMB/administrative share copy behavior and remote service execution sequences.

attack_profile.description: Internal payload staging often follows credential compromise and host discovery.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Detect uncommon host-to-host file transfer over administrative shares.

T1005.description: Adversaries may collect documents and files from compromised local systems.
T1005.detection: Track bulk access or compression of sensitive file types and directories.

attack_profile.description: Collection activity often appears as broad file enumeration followed by archival commands.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Alert on compression utilities targeting user profile and shared data paths.

T1041.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data using established command and control channels.
T1041.detection: Identify sustained outbound beacons with unusually high transfer volume per session.

attack_profile.description: Data theft can piggyback on established C2 channels to reduce detection opportunities.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Correlate beacon regularity with payload size anomalies.

T1562.description: Adversaries may disable or tamper with security tooling and logging to evade detection.
T1562.detection: Detect stop/disable operations against EDR, AV, firewall, and log services.

attack_profile.description: Defense impairment is a strong precursor to disruptive and stealthy follow-on actions.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Prioritize commands and service control events targeting defensive products.

T1136.description: Adversaries may create local or domain accounts to maintain access.
T1136.detection: Alert on new account creation, especially privileged group membership shortly after.

attack_profile.description: Persistence through account creation enables durable command and control and privilege abuse.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Correlate account creation with immediate privileged group assignments.

T1059.description: Adversaries may abuse command and scripting interpreters for execution.
T1059.detection: Track suspicious shell and script interpreter usage across hosts.

attack_profile.description: Scripting engines are dual-use and frequently observed in post-exploit operations.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Model baseline administrative automation versus unusual interactive abuse.

T1078.description: Adversaries may use valid credentials to access systems while blending with normal activity.
T1078.detection: Detect impossible travel, MFA anomalies, and atypical logon patterns.

attack_profile.description: Credential abuse is central to many intrusions and hard to distinguish from legitimate activity.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Correlate geo-velocity, device novelty, and privilege escalation post-authentication.

T1021.description: Adversaries may leverage remote services such as RDP, SMB, and WinRM for lateral movement.
T1021.detection: Detect unusual remote administration patterns and cross-segment remote execution.

attack_profile.description: Lateral movement over management protocols can indicate rapid attacker expansion.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Detect first-time remote admin protocol usage between peer endpoints.

T1087.description: Adversaries may enumerate user and service accounts to expand targeting options.
T1087.detection: Monitor account enumeration commands and identity API reconnaissance spikes.

attack_profile.description: Account discovery commonly appears in early internal reconnaissance.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Track repeated identity query commands from non-admin hosts.

T1082.description: Adversaries may query system details to inform follow-on actions.
T1082.detection: Detect bursts of host/environment discovery commands from the same process tree.

attack_profile.description: System profiling improves attacker decision-making for privilege and persistence paths.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Alert when clustered discovery commands occur outside admin maintenance windows.

T1071.description: Adversaries may use common application protocols to blend command and control traffic.
T1071.detection: Detect abnormal beaconing and low-prevalence domains over HTTP/S and DNS.

attack_profile.description: Application-layer C2 is prevalent due to protocol ubiquity and blending opportunities.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Combine periodicity analytics with destination rarity and payload size features.

T1046.description: Adversaries may scan networks to identify reachable services and hosts.
T1046.detection: Detect horizontal/vertical scanning and unusual host fan-out behaviors.

attack_profile.description: Recon scans typically precede lateral movement and remote execution attempts.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Model normal scanner assets and detect scan-like behavior from non-scanner hosts.

T1018.description: Adversaries may enumerate remote systems to identify lateral movement targets.
T1018.detection: Track host enumeration command sequences and directory service queries.

attack_profile.description: Remote discovery maps enterprise topology and prioritizes high-value systems.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Correlate endpoint command discovery with sudden network sweeps.

T1490.description: Adversaries may disable recovery features to maximize destructive impact.
T1490.detection: Alert on shadow copy, backup, and boot recovery configuration tampering.

attack_profile.description: Recovery inhibition is a hallmark of ransomware and extortion-focused campaigns.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Alert on backup/restore command usage from unusual parent processes.

T1036.description: Adversaries may disguise artifacts to appear legitimate and avoid detection.
T1036.detection: Look for mismatched file names/paths, suspicious signed binary lookalikes, and extension tricks.

attack_profile.description: Masquerading increases dwell time by reducing analyst confidence in malicious artifacts.
attack_profile.detection_hint: Detect executable naming anomalies and suspicious path-location combinations.
